context:
<Profile>### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Alex Rivera  
- **Age:** 24  
- **Gender:** non-binary  
- **Experience level:** **Novice (Beginner)** — currently rebuilding capacity post-ACL reconstruction; training age and recent consistency not provided.

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):**  
  - **Return-to-sport performance (soccer)** — safely resume soccer participation after **right ACL reconstruction**, currently **~9 months post-op** (as of **2026-02-27**)  
  - **Rebuild leg strength** (with emphasis on right/left symmetry, knee stability, and confidence)
- **Secondary goals:**  
  - Reduce re-injury risk and improve movement quality for cutting/landing mechanics  
  - Maintain consistency with gym training alongside PT
- **Target events/deadlines:** Not specified (e.g., target return-to-play date, first match, tryouts)

---

## TRAINING CONTEXT

### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** Not specified  
- **Desired training frequency:** **4 sessions/week**  
- **Session duration preferences/constraints:** Not specified  
- **Time windows:** Not specified  
- **Blocked days:** Not specified

### Equipment & Environment
- **Primary location:** Commercial gym — **University recreation center**  
- **Available equipment:**  
  - Barbells (exact plates/limits not specified)  
  - Dumbbells (range not specified)  
  - Standard strength machines (types not specified)

### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** Right knee **ACL reconstruction — ~9 months post-op**, still attending PT; increased fear/avoidance around re-injury (since **2026-02-27**). *Training should follow PT guidance/clearances; monitor swelling, pain, instability, and next-day response.*
- **[ACTIVE]** Return-to-sport risk considerations: cutting/pivoting/landing tolerance and knee confidence not yet established (since **2026-02-27**).

### Preferences
- **Likes/dislikes:** Not specified  
- **Communication style preferences:** Not specified  
- **Focus areas:** Soccer return-to-play readiness, unilateral leg strength, stability, and confidence building

---

## METRICS
*(No baseline performance metrics provided yet. Establishing starting measures is recommended for safe progression and symmetry tracking.)*

### Movement Quality (priority: ACL return-to-sport readiness)
- **Post-op status:** Right ACL reconstruction, **~9 months post-op** (as of **2026-02-27**)  
- **Pain/swelling/instability status:** Not provided  
- **Functional milestones (not yet recorded):**  
  - Single-leg squat quality (R/L)  
  - Hop tests (single/triple/crossover) symmetry  
  - Landing mechanics (video/coach/PT assessment)  
  - Knee ROM and quad strength symmetry (often tracked in PT)

### Strength
- No current lifts provided (as of **2026-02-27**)

### Endurance
- No soccer conditioning or running metrics provided (as of **2026-02-27**)

### Body Composition
- Weight/body comp not provided (as of **2026-02-27**)

---

## LOG

## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (ACL return-to-sport focus)
- Captured goals: return to soccer performance; rebuild leg strength; reduce re-injury risk.
- Training context added: **4x/week** desired frequency; commercial gym (university rec center) with barbells/dumbbells/machines.
- Constraints recorded: **Right ACL reconstruction (~9 months post-op)**, still in PT; nervous about re-injury.
- Metrics section initialized: no baseline measures provided yet; movement-quality/return-to-sport metrics to be added when available.

--- 

If you share any of the following, I can update the METRICS and constraints more precisely: current PT restrictions/clearances (running, jumping, cutting), pain/swelling levels, ROM status, and any current strength numbers (e.g., leg press, split squat, RDL, hamstring curl) plus which days/times you prefer to train.</Profile>

<Plan>## Header
- **Program name:** ACL Return-to-Soccer Strength & Confidence (4-Day Gym + Return-to-Sport Prep)
- **Program Owner:** GymText
- **User:** Alex Rivera
- **Goal:** Safe return-to-sport (soccer) after right ACL reconstruction; rebuild symmetrical leg strength, knee stability, and confidence
- **Duration:** Ongoing with phase cycling (4-week blocks)

---

## Program Philosophy
Alex is ~9 months post–ACL reconstruction and still in PT, so the program prioritizes **knee-friendly strength rebuilding, single-leg symmetry, and progressive exposure to landing/cutting demands**—without outpacing medical clearance. A **4-day split** balances consistency and recovery: two lower-body–emphasis sessions (one strength, one athletic/plyo emphasis if cleared) plus two upper/core sessions that also include targeted knee/hip/ankle “prehab.” The plan uses **simple, repeatable movement patterns** (novice-friendly) with **autoregulated intensity (RPE)** so Alex can train hard on good days and back off when the knee is reactive. Key constraints (ACL status, fear/avoidance, unknown current tolerance) are addressed by **tight technique standards, controlled eccentrics/isometrics early, gradual plyometric progressions, and clear stop rules** (pain/swelling/instability/next-day response).

---

# Phase 1: Rebuild Capacity & Symmetry (Weeks 1–4)
**Intent:** Establish consistent training, restore quad/hamstring capacity, improve single-leg control, and build confidence with controlled landings (only if cleared).

### Weekly Pattern (4 sessions/week)
Suggested days (adjust as needed): **Mon / Tue / Thu / Sat**

#### Day 1 — Lower A (Strength + Unilateral Control)
- **Focus:** Knee-dominant strength + single-leg symmetry
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets (lower body)

1) **Leg Press (feet mid-to-high, knee-friendly ROM):** 4 × 6–8 @ RPE 7  
   *Stable environment to load quads safely and rebuild confidence.*

2) **Rear-Foot-Elevated Split Squat (DBs):** 3 × 8 each side @ RPE 6–7  
   *Primary symmetry builder; keep reps clean and depth pain-free.*

3) **Seated or Lying Hamstring Curl (machine):** 3 × 10–12 @ RPE 7  
   *Hamstring strength supports tibial control and knee stability.*

4) **Step-Down (6–10" box, slow):** 2–3 × 6–8 each @ RPE 6  
   *Eccentric control + knee tracking for decel mechanics.*

5) **Standing Calf Raise:** 3 × 10–12 @ RPE 7  
6) **Pallof Press (cable/band):** 2 × 10–12 each side

---

#### Day 2 — Upper A + Trunk + Knee “Prehab”
- **Focus:** Upper strength + trunk/hip support (low knee fatigue)
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets

1) **DB Bench Press:** 3 × 8 @ RPE 7  
2) **Chest-Supported Row:** 3 × 8–10 @ RPE 7  
3) **Lat Pulldown (neutral grip if possible):** 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
4) **DB Overhead Press (seated if preferred):** 2–3 × 8 @ RPE 7  
5) **Side Plank:** 2 × 20–30s each  
6) **Hip Abduction (cable or machine):** 2–3 × 12–15 each @ RPE 7  
   *Glute med strength supports knee alignment in single-leg tasks.*

---

#### Day 3 — Lower B (Hinge + Deceleration Basics)
- **Focus:** Posterior chain + controlled decel/landing patterns
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets (lower body)

1) **Romanian Deadlift (DB or barbell):** 4 × 6–8 @ RPE 7  
   *Posterior chain strength for sprinting mechanics and knee load-sharing.*

2) **Hip Thrust (barbell or machine):** 3 × 8–10 @ RPE 7  
3) **Goblet Box Squat (box height that allows control):** 3 × 8 @ RPE 6–7  
   *Re-groove squat pattern with predictable depth and stability.*

4) **Single-Leg RDL (DB):** 2–3 × 8 each @ RPE 6–7  
5) **Tibialis Raise (machine or wall):** 2 × 12–15  
6) **Optional (ONLY if PT-cleared): Low-level landing drill**  
   - **Snap-down to stick (two-leg):** 3 × 3–5 reps  
   *Teach quiet landings, knee-over-toes tracking, and trunk control.*

---

#### Day 4 — Upper B + Conditioning Base (Knee-Friendly)
- **Focus:** Upper hypertrophy + easy aerobic base that shouldn’t flare the knee
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets + 10–20 min cardio

1) **Incline DB Press:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
2) **Cable Row:** 3 × 10–12 @ RPE 7  
3) **DB Lateral Raise:** 2–3 × 12–15 @ RPE 7–8  
4) **Face Pull:** 2–3 × 12–15 @ RPE 7  
5) **Farmer Carry:** 3 × 20–40m (moderate)  
6) **Bike or Incline Walk (Zone 2):** 10–20 min, easy conversational pace  
   *Build work capacity without high impact.*

---

## Phase 1 — Progression Strategy (Weeks 1–4)
- **RPE cap:** Keep most work **RPE 6–7** for Week 1, then nudge to **RPE 7–8** by Week 3 if knee response is good.
- **Double progression (main lifts):**  
  - Stay in the rep range (e.g., 6–8).  
  - When you hit the **top reps on all sets** at the target RPE, add **2.5–10 lb** next time (smallest jump available).
- **Unilateral symmetry rule:** Start the **right leg first**. Match reps on the left; you may add **+1 rep** on the right only if PT approves and movement quality stays perfect (no knee cave, no pain).
- **Deload (Week 4):** Reduce **sets by ~40–50%** and keep loads about the same or slightly lighter (RPE ~6).  
- **Stop rules (session or next day):** Swelling increase, sharp pain, instability/giving way, limping, or next-day pain >2/10 above baseline → reduce ROM/load next session and inform PT.

Transition to Phase 2 if: Week 3 was tolerated well (no swelling flare-ups) and PT agrees that impact/landing progressions can continue.

---

# Phase 2: Strength Build + Intro Plyometrics (Weeks 5–8)
**Intent:** Build more robust single-leg strength and begin **structured plyometric progression** (still conservative), preparing for running/soccer demands as cleared.

### Weekly Pattern
Same 4-day template.

#### Day 1 — Lower A (Quad Strength + Split Squat Progression)
- **Focus:** Heavier knee-dominant work; unilateral loading
- **Volume:** ~15–20 sets

1) **Leg Press or Hack Squat (machine):** 4 × 5–7 @ RPE 7–8  
2) **RFESS (DBs):** 4 × 6–8 each @ RPE 7–8  
3) **Leg Extension (if tolerated):** 2–3 × 10–12 @ RPE 7  
   *Direct quad work often needed post-ACL; keep pain-free and controlled.*
4) **Hamstring Curl:** 3 × 8–10 @ RPE 7–8  
5) **Calf Raise:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7–8

---

#### Day 2 — Upper A + Trunk + Hip
- **Focus:** Upper strength; trunk/hip stiffness for cutting mechanics
- **Volume:** ~12–16 sets

1) **Bench Press (barbell or DB):** 4 × 5–7 @ RPE 7–8  
2) **Row (supported):** 4 × 6–8 @ RPE 7–8  
3) **Pulldown/Pull-up Assist:** 3 × 8–10 @ RPE 7  
4) **Anti-rotation (Pallof) + Side plank superset:** 2–3 rounds

---

#### Day 3 — Lower B (Posterior Chain + Plyo “A”)
- **Focus:** Hinge strength + low-to-moderate plyometrics (if cleared)
- **Volume:** ~14–18 sets + low plyo contacts

1) **RDL (barbell if ready):** 4 × 5–7 @ RPE 7–8  
2) **Hip Thrust:** 3 × 6–8 @ RPE 7–8  
3) **Single-Leg RDL:** 3 × 6–8 each @ RPE 7  
4) **Optional (ONLY if PT-cleared): Plyo A — jump/landing basics**
   - **Pogo hops (double-leg):** 3 × 10–20 contacts  
   - **Box jump to stick (low box):** 3 × 3–5  
   *Goal is stiffness + quiet landing, not height.*
5) **Tibialis Raise:** 2 × 12–15

---

#### Day 4 — Upper B + Conditioning Progression
- **Focus:** Upper volume + aerobic base (or return-to-run supports)
- **Volume:** ~12–16 sets + 15–25 min cardio

1) **Incline Press:** 3 × 8–10 @ RPE 7  
2) **Cable Row / 1-arm row:** 3 × 10–12 @ RPE 7  
3) **Shoulders + rear delts (raise/face pull):** 4–6 total sets @ RPE 7–8  
4) **Carries:** 3 × 30–50m  
5) **Bike/Incline Walk (Zone 2):** 15–25 min  
   - If PT has cleared running: swap 1x/week for **easy run/walk intervals** per PT plan.

---

## Phase 2 — Progression Strategy (Weeks 5–8)
- **Weeks 5–7:** Add load slowly; aim to increase either **+1 rep per set** or **+2.5–10 lb** weekly on main lifts while staying within RPE targets.
- **Plyo progression (if cleared):** Increase **contacts** before intensity. Example:  
  - Week 5: 30–50 total contacts/week  
  - Week 6: 50–70  
  - Week 7: 60–90  
  Keep landings symmetrical and quiet; stop if knee confidence drops or mechanics degrade.
- **Deload (Week 8):** Reduce lifting sets ~40–50%; keep plyos very light or skip them entirely.

Transition to Phase 3 if: unilateral strength and landing mechanics are stable, and PT clears higher-intent deceleration/change-of-direction prep.

---

# Phase 3: Return-to-Sport Build (Strength Maintenance + Decel/COD Prep) (Weeks 9–12)
**Intent:** Maintain and slightly progress strength while shifting more “budget” to controlled deceleration, lateral work, and soccer-specific robustness (still gym-based; field work remains PT/coach-led).

### Weekly Pattern
Same 4-day structure, with slightly reduced lifting volume on lower days to make room for athletic work.

#### Day 1 — Lower A (Strength Maintenance + Power Primer)
- **Focus:** Keep strength while adding low-fatigue power
- **Volume:** ~12–16 sets + small power dose

1) **Trap Bar Deadlift (if available) OR Leg Press:** 3–4 × 4–6 @ RPE 7–8  
   *Trap bar often feels more stable and knee-friendly than straight-bar pulling.*

2) **RFESS:** 3 × 6–8 each @ RPE 7–8  
3) **Hamstring Curl:** 3 × 8–10 @ RPE 7–8  
4) **Power primer (ONLY if cleared): Med ball scoop toss OR light jump to stick:** 3 × 3–5

---

#### Day 2 — Upper A + Trunk
- **Focus:** Upper strength; trunk stiffness
- **Volume:** ~12–16 sets

(Keep similar to Phase 2, progress slowly.)

---

#### Day 3 — Lower B (Decel/Lateral Emphasis + Posterior Chain)
- **Focus:** Braking mechanics, lateral capacity, single-leg control
- **Volume:** ~10–14 sets + drills

1) **RDL:** 3 × 5–7 @ RPE 7–8  
2) **Lateral Lunge (DB):** 3 × 6–8 each @ RPE 6–7  
   *Build frontal-plane strength for cutting tolerance.*

3) **Step-Down or Single-Leg Squat to Box:** 2–3 × 5–8 each @ RPE 6–7  
4) **Optional (ONLY if cleared): Decel/COD drill menu (low volume)**
   - **Snap-down to stick:** 2 × 3–5  
   - **Lateral bound to stick (small):** 2–3 × 3 each  
   - **5–10m build-up → stick stop:** 4–6 reps  
   *Quality > intensity; you should finish feeling better, not “wrecked.”*

---

#### Day 4 — Upper B + Conditioning
- **Focus:** Upper volume + aerobic base (or return-to-run progression)
- **Volume:** ~12–16 sets + cardio

Cardio options (choose based on PT status):
- If not cleared for running: **bike/elliptical Zone 2 (20–30 min)**
- If cleared: **easy run** OR **interval walk/run** per PT plan

---

## Phase 3 — Progression Strategy (Weeks 9–12)
- **Strength:** Maintain or small PRs via rep progression (don’t chase maxes). Keep main lifts mostly **RPE 7–8**.
- **Decel/COD:** Increase complexity in this order:
  1) Stick landings →  
  2) Slightly faster approaches →  
  3) Small lateral bounds →  
  4) Planned COD patterns (still controlled)
- **Deload (Week 12):** Reduce lifting volume 40–50% and keep athletic drills very light.

---

## Phase Cycling
**Default cycle:** **Phase 1 → Phase 2 → Phase 3 → repeat**, with 1 deload week every 4th week.
- If the knee becomes reactive (swelling/pain/instability) or life stress/sleep is poor: **drop back one phase** (or run the deload week early).
- If PT clears higher-level tasks (running → jumping → cutting), progress the athletic components first by **volume and quality**, not by intensity.
- If Alex has a defined return-to-play date later, this can shift into a more formal **8–12 week “return-to-sport peak”** with planned field sessions; for now, the program stays conservative and PT-compatible.

**Recommended check-ins (every 4 weeks):**
- Single-leg squat to box (video from front/side): knee tracking + trunk control
- Step-down quality (R/L)
- Unilateral loading tolerance (RFESS loads/reps each side)
- Next-day knee response after Lower B sessions

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created based on profile (ACL RTS focus, 4x/week, novice-friendly autoregulation, PT-first constraints).

--- 

If you tell me (1) which days you prefer to train, and (2) what your PT has cleared (running? jumping? cutting?), I’ll lock in exact weekly scheduling and tighten the plyo/COD menu to match your current stage.</Plan>

reference.response:
# Microcycle — Week of 2026-02-23

**Program:** ACL Return-to-Soccer Strength & Confidence (4-Day Gym + Return-to-Sport Prep)  
**Phase:** Phase 1 — Rebuild Capacity & Symmetry (Week 1 of 4)  
**User:** Alex Rivera  

## Schedule
- **Mon (2/23):** Lower A — Strength + Unilateral Control (university rec center)
- **Tue (2/24):** Upper A — Upper Strength + Trunk + Knee “Prehab” (university rec center)
- **Wed (2/25):** Rest / PT / easy walk
- **Thu (2/26):** Lower B — Hinge + Deceleration Basics (university rec center)
- **Fri (2/27):** Rest / PT
- **Sat (2/28):** Upper B — Upper Volume + Knee-Friendly Conditioning (university rec center)
- **Sun (3/1):** Rest

## Week Overview
Week 1 is an “on-ramp” week: keep most work **RPE 6–7**, prioritize **perfect reps**, and use this week to establish starting loads you can repeat and build on. Since your PT clearance for running/jumping/cutting isn’t specified, **no plyometrics/cutting drills are programmed this week**—we’ll keep it strength + control + confidence. Start **right leg first** on unilateral work, match the left to the right, and follow stop rules (sharp pain, swelling increase, instability/giving way, limping, or next-day pain >2/10 above baseline).

---

# Workout — Monday, February 23, 2026
**Focus:** Lower A (Knee-dominant strength + single-leg symmetry)  
**Location:** University recreation center  
**Duration:** ~80 minutes  

## Warm-Up (12 minutes)
1. **Bike (easy):** 5:00 at conversational pace  
2. **Dynamic prep (2 rounds):**  
   - Leg swings front/back: × 10/side  
   - Leg swings side/side: × 10/side  
   - Hip circles: × 8/side  
3. **Activation / patterning (2 rounds):**  
   - Glute bridge: × 10 (2-sec squeeze)  
   - Bodyweight box squat to comfortable depth: × 8  
   - Terminal knee extension (band or cable): × 12/side (smooth, full lockout)

## Main Workout

### 1. Leg Press (feet mid-to-high)
**Target:** 4 × 6 @ RPE 6–7 (knee-friendly ROM; no pain pinch at bottom)  
- **Set 1:** 70 lb × 10 (warm-up, easy)  
- **Set 2:** 90 lb × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 110 lb × 6 (RPE 6)  
- **Set 4:** 110 lb × 6 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 5:** 110 lb × 6 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 6:** 110 lb × 6 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 2–3 min between working sets  
**Notes:** Control the lowering (2 seconds). Track knee over mid-foot; stop 1–2 inches before any pinch/shift.

### 2. Rear-Foot-Elevated Split Squat (DB)
**Target:** 3 × 8/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** Bodyweight × 6/side (warm-up; find stance)  
- **Set 2:** 15 lb DBs (30 total) × 8/side (RPE 6)  
- **Set 3:** 15 lb DBs × 8/side (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 4:** 15 lb DBs × 8/side (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** **Right leg first.** Slight forward torso is OK. Front foot tripod; knee tracks over 2nd–3rd toe; no knee cave.

### 3. Seated Hamstring Curl (machine)
**Target:** 3 × 12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 30 lb × 12 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 40 lb × 12 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 3:** 40 lb × 12 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 4:** 40 lb × 12 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 75–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Smooth reps, 1-sec squeeze at curl; don’t let hips pop up.

### 4. Step-Down (6" box, slow)
**Target:** 2 × 6/side @ RPE 6 (3-sec lower)  
- **Set 1:** Bodyweight × 6/side (RPE 5–6)  
- **Set 2:** Bodyweight × 6/side (RPE 6)  
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Light fingertip support on a rail is allowed if it improves knee tracking. Goal = quiet, controlled descent.

### 5. Standing Calf Raise
**Target:** 3 × 12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 50 lb × 12 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 70 lb × 12 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 3:** 70 lb × 12 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 4:** 70 lb × 12 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Full stretch at bottom (1 sec), full rise at top (1 sec).

### 6. Pallof Press (cable)
**Target:** 2 × 12/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 15 lb × 12/side  
- **Set 2:** 15 lb × 12/side  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Exhale as you press out; don’t rotate.

## Cool Down (8 minutes)
1. Quad stretch (standing or couch stretch): 30 sec/side × 2  
2. Hamstring stretch: 30 sec/side × 2  
3. Calf stretch: 30 sec/side × 1  
4. Easy bike or walk: 2:00

## Notes
- Session intent is **confidence + symmetry**, not chasing load.  
- If knee feels “full” later today or tomorrow: note it and reduce ROM/load Thursday.

---

# Workout — Tuesday, February 24, 2026
**Focus:** Upper A (Upper strength + trunk + hip support)  
**Location:** University recreation center  
**Duration:** ~70 minutes  

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. **Row erg or bike:** 4:00 easy  
2. **Shoulder prep (2 rounds):**  
   - Band pull-aparts: × 15  
   - Scap push-ups: × 10  
   - Cable or band external rotation (elbow at side): × 12/side  
3. **Trunk primer:** Dead bug × 6/side (slow)

## Main Workout

### 1. DB Bench Press
**Target:** 3 × 8 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 20 lb DBs × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 25 lb DBs × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 30 lb DBs × 8 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 4:** 30 lb DBs × 8 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 5:** 30 lb DBs × 8 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 2 min between working sets  
**Notes:** Feet planted; steady tempo; stop 1 rep before grind.

### 2. Chest-Supported Row (machine or incline bench DB row)
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 40 lb × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 60 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 3:** 60 lb × 10 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 4:** 60 lb × 10 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Pull elbows toward hips; pause 1 sec at the top.

### 3. Lat Pulldown (neutral grip if available)
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 60 lb × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 70 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 3:** 70 lb × 10 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 4:** 70 lb × 10 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Ribcage down; avoid swinging.

### 4. Seated DB Overhead Press
**Target:** 2 × 8 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 15 lb DBs × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 20 lb DBs × 8 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 3:** 20 lb DBs × 8 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Control down; don’t flare ribs.

### 5. Side Plank
**Target:** 2 × 25 sec/side  
- **Set 1:** 25 sec/side  
- **Set 2:** 25 sec/side  
**Rest:** 45 sec between sides/sets  
**Notes:** Straight line head-to-heel; top hip slightly forward.

### 6. Hip Abduction (machine)
**Target:** 3 × 15 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 50 lb × 15 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 70 lb × 15 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 3:** 70 lb × 15 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 4:** 70 lb × 15 (RPE 7–7.5)  
**Rest:** 60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Slight forward lean; no bouncing.

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. Doorway pec stretch: 30 sec/side × 2  
2. Lat stretch (bench or pole): 30 sec/side × 2  
3. Easy walk: 2:00

## Notes
- Keep this day “knee-light.” If PT gave you any daily quad activation, do it after this session.

---

# Workout — Thursday, February 26, 2026
**Focus:** Lower B (Posterior chain + controlled squat pattern)  
**Location:** University recreation center  
**Duration:** ~85 minutes  

## Warm-Up (12 minutes)
1. **Bike:** 4:00 easy  
2. **Hinge + ankle prep (2 rounds):**  
   - Hip hinge drill with dowel: × 8  
   - Ankle rocks (knee to wall): × 10/side  
   - Bodyweight RDL (hands on hips): × 10  
3. **Glute/ham activation:**  
   - Banded lateral walk: × 10 steps each way  
   - Glute bridge: × 8 (2-sec squeeze)

## Main Workout

### 1. Romanian Deadlift (barbell)
**Target:** 4 × 8 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 45 lb × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 65 lb × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 85 lb × 8 (RPE 6)  
- **Set 4:** 85 lb × 8 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 5:** 85 lb × 8 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 6:** 85 lb × 8 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 2–3 min between working sets  
**Notes:** Soft knees, shins mostly vertical, bar close. Stop if you lose hamstring tension.

### 2. Hip Thrust (barbell)
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 65 lb × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 95 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 3:** 95 lb × 10 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 4:** 95 lb × 10 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 2 min between sets  
**Notes:** Full lockout, 1-sec squeeze; chin tucked; ribs down.

### 3. Goblet Box Squat (box height you can control)
**Target:** 3 × 8 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** Bodyweight × 8 (warm-up; find box height)  
- **Set 2:** 25 lb DB × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 35 lb DB × 8 (RPE 6)  
- **Set 4:** 35 lb DB × 8 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 5:** 35 lb DB × 8 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between working sets  
**Notes:** Light tap to box—don’t “plop.” Knees track; even pressure through whole foot.

### 4. Single-Leg RDL (DB)
**Target:** 2 × 8/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 20 lb DB × 8/side (RPE 6)  
- **Set 2:** 20 lb DB × 8/side (RPE 6.5–7)  
**Rest:** 60–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** **Right leg first.** Hips square; reach back with the free leg; use a light fingertip support if balance limits quality.

### 5. Tibialis Raise (machine or wall)
**Target:** 2 × 15 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 10 lb × 15  
- **Set 2:** 10 lb × 15  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Full dorsiflexion; controlled lower.

## Cool Down (8 minutes)
1. Hip flexor stretch: 30 sec/side × 2  
2. Hamstring stretch: 30 sec/side × 2  
3. Adductor rock-backs: × 8 slow reps  
4. Easy walk: 2:00

## Notes
- This is your “posterior chain confidence” day—leave the gym feeling better than you came in.  
- If any next-day knee irritation shows up, next week we’ll adjust squat depth/volume first.

# Workout — Saturday, February 28, 2026
**Focus:** Upper B (Upper volume + easy aerobic base)  
**Location:** University recreation center  
**Duration:** ~75 minutes  

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. **Bike:** 4:00 easy  
2. **Upper + posture circuit (2 rounds):**  
   - Face pull (light): × 12  
   - Incline push-up or bench push-up: × 8  
   - Band pull-apart: × 15  
3. **Carry warm-up:** Farmer hold (light) 20 sec

## Main Workout

### 1. Incline DB Press
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 20 lb DBs × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 25 lb DBs × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 30 lb DBs × 10 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 4:** 30 lb DBs × 10 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 5:** 30 lb DBs × 10 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between working sets  
**Notes:** Don’t flare elbows; steady descent.

### 2. Cable Row
**Target:** 3 × 12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 50 lb × 12 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 70 lb × 12 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 3:** 70 lb × 12 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 4:** 70 lb × 12 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 75–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** 1-sec squeeze; no torso rocking.

### 3. DB Lateral Raise
**Target:** 3 × 15 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** 10 lb DBs × 15 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 2:** 10 lb DBs × 15 (RPE 7.5)  
- **Set 3:** 10 lb DBs × 15 (RPE 8)  
**Rest:** 60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Slight lean forward; raise to just below shoulder height.

### 4. Face Pull (cable)
**Target:** 3 × 15 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb × 15 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 2:** 30 lb × 15 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 3:** 30 lb × 15 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Pull to eyebrows; elbows high; squeeze upper back.

### 5. Farmer Carry (DBs)
**Target:** 3 × 30 m (moderate, clean posture)  
- **Set 1:** 40 lb DBs × 30 m  
- **Set 2:** 40 lb DBs × 30 m  
- **Set 3:** 45 lb DBs × 30 m  
**Rest:** 60–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Tall posture; ribs stacked over pelvis; smooth steps.

### 6. Bike (Zone 2)
**Target:** 15:00 easy conversational pace  
- **Work:** 15:00 continuous @ “could speak in full sentences”  
**Notes:** Knee should feel warmed, not irritated. Keep resistance moderate.

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. Pec stretch: 30 sec/side × 2  
2. Upper trap/neck gentle stretch: 20 sec/side × 2  
3. Easy walk: 2:00

## Notes
- Conditioning is deliberately **easy** this week—goal is capacity without knee flare.

## Weekly Summary
- **Lower A (Mon):** Established baseline knee-dominant loading with **leg press 110×6×4 (RPE ~6–7)** and clean unilateral work (**RFESS 15s×8/side**).  
- **Upper A (Tue):** Solid foundational upper work at controlled effort (**DB bench 30s×8×3; pulldown 70×10×3**) plus hip abduction for knee alignment support.  
- **Lower B (Thu):** Posterior chain base set with **RDL 85×8×4** and controlled squat patterning (**goblet box squat 35×8×3**)—no plyos this week due to unknown clearance.  
- **Upper B + conditioning (Sat):** Volume + posture work, finished with **15 min Zone 2 bike** to build aerobic base knee-safely.  
- **Constraint monitoring (ACL):** This week is the test week—track **pain (0–10), swelling/fullness, instability, and next-day response** after both lower sessions.  
- **Next week:** If knee response is calm (no swelling increase; next-day pain ≤ baseline +2/10), progress via **+1 rep per set** on main lifts *or* **+5–10 lb** where RPE stays ≤7. If you tell me your PT clearance (running/jumping/cutting), I’ll add the appropriate **landing/plyo “starter” drill** in Week 2.
